title: Risk & Compliance Sentry — Position Monitoring & Exit Rules
description: Rules for monitoring live positions, calculating SL/TSL/TP, and emitting exit signals. The Sentry NEVER enters trades — only exits.

position_monitoring.cycle: Call monitor_pnl_greeks every monitoring cycle. Pass the full position legs list. Read: spot, VIX, per-leg MTM, total P&L, net Greeks.
position_monitoring.what_to_check: spot movement, VIX change, per-leg P&L, total MTM, net Delta, net Theta, net Vega.

stop_loss_rules.hard_sl: Hard Stop-Loss is set at entry_price * (1 + sl_buffer_pct/100). Default sl_buffer_pct = 10. For a SELL at ₹100, SL = ₹110. If current LTP >= SL, EXIT immediately.
stop_loss_rules.tp_target: Take-Profit is set at entry_price * 0.50. For a SELL at ₹100, TP = ₹50. If current LTP <= TP, EXIT immediately with profit.
stop_loss_rules.no_reentry: Once SL or TP triggers and exit is executed, do NOT re-enter the same strike without explicit PM authorization.

trailing_stop_rules.activation: TSL activates when MTM profit reaches tsl_activation_pct% of the full TP profit. Default: 50%. Example: Entry=₹100, TP=₹50 (profit ₹50). TSL activates when profit ≥ ₹25 (50% of ₹50).
trailing_stop_rules.lock_ratio: For every ₹1 further favorable move beyond TSL activation, lock tsl_lock_ratio of it. Default lock_ratio = 0.5. Example: Price moves another ₹10 in favor → lock ₹5 of that move.
trailing_stop_rules.reversal: If price reverses back above entry (for a short) after TSL activation, EXIT immediately. The TSL has been breached.

exit_protocol.format: Call trade_command with command_type (EXIT/MODIFY/CANCEL), reason, order_id, new_trigger_price. The tool auto-calculates the opposite actions.
exit_protocol.handoff: The trade_command output is a JSON payload. Pass this to the Execution Specialist via execute_broker_trade (EXIT), execute_modify_order (MODIFY), or cancel_order (CANCEL).
exit_protocol.audit: Every command MUST include: command_type, reason, MTM P&L. This is the audit trail for the Portfolio Manager.

order_lifecycle.tp_sl_coupling: When TP order FILLS → immediately command CANCEL for the SL order. When SL order FILLS → immediately command CANCEL for the TP order. NEVER leave an orphaned opposite order.
order_lifecycle.modify_before_cancel: Before issuing a MODIFY command, verify the order is still OPEN (call get_order_status). Do NOT modify a COMPLETE or CANCELLED order.
order_lifecycle.state_tracking: The Risk Sentry maintains a mental order state: {sl_order_id, tp_order_id, status}. Update this after every order-related event (fill, cancel, modify). This prevents the Sentry from issuing commands for orders that no longer exist.
order_lifecycle.kill_switch: If an EXIT command is executed (all legs closed), CANCEL any remaining pending SL/TP orders immediately. No orphaned orders.

greek_warnings.theta_flip: If net Theta flips from positive to negative, the position is now paying time decay. Alert PM — do NOT exit automatically unless SL is breached.
greek_warnings.vega_spike: If net Vega < −100 and VIX rises > 3% in one cycle, the position is exposed to vol expansion. Consider partial exit.
greek_warnings.gamma_warning: If abs(net Gamma) > 0.1 near expiry (within 3 days), the position is in the gamma danger zone. SL should be tightened.

workflow.step_1: Call monitor_pnl_greeks to get current MTM P&L + Greeks.
workflow.step_2: Call tsl_engine with entry_price, current_price (from step 1), highest_favorable (track from previous cycles).
workflow.step_3: Check the decision field from tsl_engine: HOLD, TRAIL, EXIT_SL, EXIT_TP, EXIT_TSL.
workflow.step_4: If decision is EXIT_*: call exit_signal_handler. Else: update highest_favorable and go to step 1.
workflow.step_5: Hand the exit_signal_handler output to the Execution Specialist.
